attachment.snippet:
1	# Contributing to Skill Forge
2	
3	SKF turns code and docs into verified agent skills. Every instruction traces back to a real line of source. See [README.md](README.md) for the pitch; this file covers how to land changes without setting the test suite on fire.
4	
5	SKF is a [BMAD](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD) module. For BMAD philosophy, framework conventions, and module-authoring patterns in general, start at [docs.bmad-method.org](https://docs.bmad-method.org). This doc stays scoped to what's SKF-specific.
6	
7	## What You Can Contribute
8	
9	- **Workflows** (`src/skf-*/`) — new skill-compilation or lifecycle flows. Example: a `skf-diff-skill` that compares two versions of the same skill and emits a migration note.
10	- **Knowledge fragments** (`src/knowledge/`) — cross-workflow principles Ferris loads just-in-time. Example: a new `security-review.md` that captures rules reused by CS, QS, and AS.
11	- **Forger assets** (`src/forger/`, `src/shared/`) — shared agent memory, preferences, or helpers (e.g. tier detection, health-check templates).
12	- **Validators** (`tools/validate-*.js`) — deterministic checks that run in `npm run quality`. Example: a new validator that flags `{installed_path}` leaks in step files.
13	- **Docs** (`docs/`, `website/`) — tutorial / reference / explanation content surfaced at [armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/).
14	- **Ecosystem integrations** — new tool bridges (ast-grep, cocoindex, QMD, tessl, Snyk, graphify-style indexers) wired through the tier-aware discovery path.
15	- **Bug reports** — always useful, especially if they come in via the workflow health-check loop (see below).
16	
17	If you're not sure where a change belongs, open an issue and ask before writing code.
18	
19	## Local Setup
20	
21	**Platforms:** Linux, Windows, and macOS. Linux and Windows are exercised in CI on every PR (`ubuntu-latest` + `windows-latest` matrix); macOS works in practice (POSIX-equivalent to Linux) but isn't CI-gated. On Windows, SKF transparently falls back to NTFS junctions when symlink privilege isn't held — no Developer Mode or admin rights required. Git Bash (bundled with [Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win)), PowerShell, and WSL2 all work.
22	
23	**Prerequisites:**
24	
25	- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) >= 22 — the supported floor (`engines.node`); development and CI run Node 24 (see `.nvmrc`)
26	- [Python](https://www.python.org/) >= 3.10
27	- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) — runs the Python test suite
28	- `git`, `gh` — used by several workflows and by the health-check loop
29	
30	```bash
31	git clone https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.git
32	cd bmad-module-skill-forge
33	npm install           # also wires husky pre-commit hooks via "prepare"
34	npm run quality       # run the full local pre-flight
35	```
36	
37	The `npm run quality` script is your contract with CI. It runs:
38	
39	- `format:check` (Prettier), `lint` (ESLint), `lint:md` (markdownlint)
40	- `test:schemas`, `test:install`, `test:cli`, `test:workflow`, `test:python`, `test:knowledge`
41	- `validate:schemas`, `validate:skills`, `validate:refs`
42	- `docs:validate-drift` — SKF docs vs. the canonical [oh-my-skills](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/oh-my-skills) output
43	
44	If `npm run quality` passes locally, CI should too. The same steps run in [`.github/workflows/quality.yaml`](.github/workflows/quality.yaml) on every pull request.
45	
46	## Workflow for Changes
47	
48	1. **Branch from `main`.** Name it like the commit scope: `fix/skf-test-skill-...`, `feat/health-check-...`, `docs/...`.
49	2. **Match the commit-message convention from the git log.** SKF uses conventional-commit prefixes with a scoped subsystem:
50	   - `feat(skf-create-skill): ...`
51	   - `fix(health-check): ...`
52	   - `docs(readme): ...`
53	   - `ci(health-check): ...`
54	   - `refactor(skf-create-skill): ...`
55	   - `chore: ...` (no scope needed)
56	
57	   `git log --oneline -20` is the authoritative style guide. Match what you see.
58	
59	3. **Reference issues with `Fixes #NNN`** in the PR body (and optionally in the commit trailer). Use **same-repo GitHub issue numbers only** — do not reference internal IDs under `_bmad-output/todo/` or elsewhere; those are author notes, not public contracts.
60	4. **Pre-commit hooks run automatically** via husky + lint-staged: `eslint --fix`, `prettier --write`, and `markdownlint-cli2` on `.md` files. They run on staged files only.
61	5. **PR description:** explain _why_. What was broken, what does this change, and how did you verify it? Keep it honest and short. The template in [.github/](.github/) is a starting point; ignore the sections that don't apply.
62	6. **If you used Claude (or any AI assistant)** to help write a non-trivial chunk of the change, add a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer to the commit — SKF's recent history uses the format:
63	
64	   ```
65	   Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
66	   ```
67	
68	   Not mandatory, but we prefer accurate attribution over silent ghostwriting.
69	
70	## The Quality Gate
71	
72	`npm run quality` must pass before you push. If it fails:
73	
74	- **Fix the root cause.** Do not `git commit --no-verify`. Do not disable a rule to make the linter shut up. If a hook is wrong, fix the hook in a separate PR.
75	- **If a Python test fails on your machine but not in CI,** check your `uv` version and re-run `npm run test:python` from a clean shell.
76	- **If `docs:validate-drift` fails,** you either touched a pinned version/commit SHA that no longer resolves in [oh-my-skills](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/oh-my-skills), or you added a library reference the whitelist doesn't cover. Fix the reference; don't relax the validator unless the fix is clearly out of scope.
77	
78	CI re-runs everything on the PR. A green local run and a red CI run means either (a) you have uncommitted files, or (b) your Node/uv versions drift from `.nvmrc` / `test:python`. Check both before filing a CI bug.
79	
80	## Releasing
81	
82	Maintainers only — if you're not cutting a release, skip this section.
83	
84	- **Canonical path:** `.github/workflows/release.yaml`, triggered via GitHub Actions → Run workflow → choose `version_bump` (`alpha` / `beta` / `rc` / `patch` / `minor` / `major`). That is the only supported route — OIDC-backed publish, required-reviewer gate on the `release` environment, auto-provenance on the npm tarball.
85	
86	See [docs/\_internal/RELEASING.md](docs/_internal/RELEASING.md) for the full procedure — branch-protection rules, the `release` environment with its required-reviewer gate, npm Trusted Publisher registration, and the seven-scenario [rollback playbook](docs/_internal/RELEASING.md#rollback-playbook).
87	
88	## Adding a New Workflow Skill
89	
90	The `src/skf-*/` directories each follow the same shape:
91	
92	```
93	src/skf-<name>/
94	  SKILL.md            # frontmatter (name, description, "Use when ..."), stages table
95	  references/            # one file per step, loaded one-at-a-time by Ferris
96	  references/         # step-scoped rules, protocols, decision tables
97	  assets/             # step-scoped templates, schemas, output formats
98	```
99	
100	- **Start from an existing skill** with similar shape — `skf-quick-skill` is the simplest, `skf-create-skill` is the reference for the full pipeline.
101	- **Or scaffold with BMAD tooling** — the `bmad-workflow-builder` skill builds / edits / converts workflows interactively; `@Ferris CS` (skf-create-skill) is the content-extraction pattern SKF uses for its own skills in the wild.
102	- **Frontmatter matters.** `validate:skills` enforces SKILL-01 through STEP-07 (see [`tools/validate-skills.js`](tools/validate-skills.js)): SKILL.md must have `name` + `description` with a "Use when" / "Use if" trigger; step files must not have `name`/`description`; step count must be 2–10; step filenames must match `step-NN-<slug>.md`.
103	- **Manifest.** Agent-facing skills (e.g. `skf-forger`) require a `bmad-skill-manifest.yaml`. Copy the one from `src/skf-forger/` and adapt.

Suite's still running — I'll commit when it goes green and then check with you before pushing. Here's the prepared change on branch `ci-node24-actions`:

## Node 24 readiness bump (staged, awaiting test suite)

**Why now**: GitHub forces actions onto Node 24 starting **June 16, 2026**; every JS action this repo pins was verified (via each tag's `action.yml`) to run `node20`.

| Change | Detail |
|---|---|
| `actions/setup-node` v4→**v6** (8 sites) | v5/v6 auto-cache breaking change is a no-op for us — `packageManager` is null, `cache: npm` already explicit in release.yaml |
| `actions/checkout` v4→**v5** (14 sites) | v5 is the pure node24 bump; deliberately *not* v6 — its creds-to-separate-file change touches release.yaml's `git push` path (lines 286/707) and deserves its own validated PR |
| `actions/github-script` v7→**v9** | dedup script audited: only injected `context`/`core`/`github.rest`, no `require('@actions/github')` |
| `softprops/action-gh-release` v2→**v3** | v3 is exactly the node24 move, nothing else |
| `astral-sh/setup-uv` v6→**v8.2.0** (exact pin) | node24 since v7; v8 stopped publishing floating major tags entirely, so exact-version pinning is mandatory going forward |
| `actions/upload-pages-artifact` v3→**v5**, `deploy-pages` v4→**v5** | docs deploy; verified no dotfiles in `website/public` so the v4 hidden-file exclusion can't bite |
| `.nvmrc` 22→**24** | active LTS for dev/CI; `engines.node >=22` untouched (raising the floor = breaking per STABILITY.md, right after v2.0.0 shipped) |
| install-smoke stays Node **22** | repurposed as the explicit `engines` **floor probe** — otherwise bumping it in lockstep would leave Node 22 support with zero test coverage anywhere; comment rewritten to document the new coupling |
| CONTRIBUTING.md | prerequisite line now distinguishes the >=22 floor from the Node 24 dev version |

Prettier passes; actionlint isn't installed locally, but the workflows are syntax-validated by GitHub on push. The ruleset's 7 required check names and the `NPM_TOKEN: ""` ×2 invariant are untouched.
